proposed_description: Correlative negation: non ... né ... né (Non bevo né caffè né tè), and preverbal Né X né Y dropping non (Né Marco né Luca sono venuti). Also single non ... né ('not ... or'): Non ho fame né sete. Governs the same concord/drop logic as the core.concord leaf but on coordinated elements.

proposed_attributes.canonical_example: Non bevo né caffè né tè.
proposed_attributes.common_miss: Non bevo o caffè o tè (calquing 'either...or'); or keeping non with a preverbal Né...né subject.

rationale: Not in the ratified 11-node tree and not needed to author any of the 8 ratified leaves, so left out of the delivered batch. But né...né is squarely negation (correlative negative conjunction) and A2-B1 core; it was the one obvious hole I hit when scoping. OWNERSHIP, updated 2026-07-14 after the connective tree landed: connective as scaffolded has NO correlative-coordination slot (its 14 nodes are cause / result / contrast / time / discourse sequencers), so the seam I flagged at v2 is now a binary choice: ratify this under negation, or open a correlative leaf under connective and I stay off it. Author's read: negation is the better home, because the diagnostic (Non bevo né caffè né tè keeps non; Né Marco né Luca sono venuti drops it) is the same concord/drop rule negation.core.concord already owns, not connector choice. Deferring the final call to Architecture; if ratified here I'll author it next pass (est. 4-5 grammar + 2 translation).
